message:
fix(session): harden SessionStoreV2 index rebuild against truncated trailing frames

The index rebuild reads segment files line-by-line to reconstruct the
offset index. If the process was interrupted mid-write, the final line
of a segment can be a truncated JSON fragment that fails to parse.
Previously this was a hard error that prevented the session from opening.

Now the rebuild detects when a parse error occurs on the very last line
of a segment file AND that line lacks a trailing newline (indicating an
incomplete write). When detected:
- Log a warning with segment path, line number, and parse error
- Truncate the segment file to the byte offset of the last valid frame
  so subsequent reads and appends remain consistent
- Continue building the index from remaining segments

Also:
- Switch from BufReader::lines() (which strips newlines and allocates
  per line) to BufReader::read_line() with a reused String buffer for
  lower allocation overhead during large segment scans
- Track line numbers for better error diagnostics
- Trim trailing \r\n before JSON parsing for cross-platform robustness
- Classify IO errors (UnexpectedEof, InvalidData) as recoverable in
  is_recoverable_index_error so the auto-recovery path handles
  filesystem-level corruption gracefully

Tests:
- create_recovers_when_index_bounds_are_corrupt: corrupt byteLength in
  index JSON, verify full recovery with correct entry count and IDs
- create_recovers_when_index_frame_metadata_is_corrupt: corrupt entryId
  in index JSON, verify integrity after rebuild
- create_recovers_when_segment_has_truncated_trailing_frame: truncate
  last segment line mid-JSON, verify recovery drops only the truncated
  frame, file is trimmed to valid boundary, and remaining entries intact

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
